mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound signals persistently accommodative monetary policy that keeps the equity discount rate compressed, supporting positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05) when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a critical threshold indicating labor market deterioration, equity markets price in recession risk and demand a higher risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing 9.0% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer demand strength that feeds through to corporate earnings growth over a quarterly horizon, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns; neither series alone is sufficient without the other confirming the demand pulse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (t-test p>0.05) when both triggers are jointly active, tested over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating industrial production growth signals capacity constraints and margin compression that precede equity market weakness over a one-month horizon, but this relationship is muted or reversed during early-cycle recoveries where INDPRO gains reflect healthy restocking.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO deceleration are positive in more than 55% of late-cycle instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year window restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broadening labor income growth that supports consumption and corporate revenue over a quarterly horizon, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as an always-on trend effect independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 6-month PAYEMS growth and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in headline CPI beyond a critical monthly threshold raises market expectations of tighter future monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a CPI MoM print above 0.4% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing upswing regime, rising housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of durable-goods demand and construction employment, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon; this relationship weakens or inverts during housing downturns where new starts reflect oversupply correction rather than demand strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST level/growth and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 during identified housing upswing periods over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint occurrence of elevated unemployment and near-zero federal funds rate signals a policy-constrained labor slack regime in which further monetary easing has diminished marginal impact on risk appetite, causing muted or negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; the effect requires both conditions simultaneously since either alone is not diagnostic of policy-trap dynamics.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint UNRATE>8% and FEDFUNDS<0.5% periods are significantly positive (t-test p<0.05) contradicting the predicted negative mechanism, tested over available historical episodes.
